title: Dedication - To My Wife
content:
Take, dear, my little sheaf of songs,
    For, old or new,
    All that is good in them belongs
    Only to you;

    And, singing as when all was young,
    They will recall
    Those others, lived but left unsung -
    The bent of all.
    W. E. H
    APRIL 1888
    SEPTEMBER 1897.
meaning:
"Dedication - To My Wife" is a quintessential example of William Ernest Henley's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
